key_insight: A hybrid UNet-Transformer can learn to map composite microstructure geometry images directly to stress and strain fields, acting as a fast surrogate for expensive FEM simulations.
engineering_relevance: This enables rapid field prediction for composite design and screening, supporting physics-informed optimization when repeated finite-element solves are too costly.
method: Input: geometry images of two-phase composite microstructures
Model: UNet encoder-decoder with Transformer attention for field translation
Output: multiple stress/strain field types across several tessellations and boundary conditions
evidence_signal: The model reports very strong accuracy on most subdatasets, with peak performance up to R2=0.9991 and SSIM=0.9936, while six of eight subdatasets achieve MAE below 0.05 on normalized pixel scale.
watch_item: Performance drops on irregular square-grid cases with sparse soft-phase inclusions, indicating difficulty capturing sharp stress discontinuities.
paper_title: Hybrid Unet-Transformer Model for Generating Stress and Strain Fields from Composite Geometrics
